name: agentdb-persistent-memory-patterns
description: Implement persistent memory patterns for AI agents using AgentDB - session memory, long-term storage, pattern learning, and context management for stateful agents, chat systems, and intelligent assistants
capabilities: Implement session memory with TTL management
Optimize memory retrieval for sub-50ms performance
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing implementation guides and TypeScript code examples for AgentDB memory management. No executable code, network calls, or file system access. Static findings are false positives from pattern-matching scanner misinterpreting documentation text as security threats.
valueStatement: AI agents forget everything between sessions. This skill shows you how to build persistent memory systems using AgentDB, enabling your agents to remember conversations, learn patterns, and maintain context across interactions.
userTitle: Build AI Agent Memory Systems with AgentDB
limitations: Requires AgentDB database setup and configuration
Basic TypeScript or JavaScript knowledge needed
Memory capacity depends on AgentDB configuration

useCases.title: Stateful Chatbots
useCases.description: Build chatbots that remember user preferences and conversation history across sessions

useCases.title: Learning Agents
useCases.description: Create agents that learn from interactions and improve performance over time

useCases.title: Context-Aware Systems
useCases.description: Design systems that maintain context across multiple interactions and workflows

promptTemplates.title: Memory Architecture Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me design a memory architecture for my AI agent using AgentDB. I need episodic memory for conversations, semantic memory for facts, and procedural memory for learned skills.

promptTemplates.title: Session Memory Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to implement session memory in AgentDB that stores conversation history and expires after 24 hours of inactivity.

promptTemplates.title: Pattern Learning Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: I want my agent to learn from user interactions. How do I implement pattern learning with clustering in AgentDB?

promptTemplates.title: Memory Optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: My agent memory queries are slow. Help me optimize retrieval speed with caching and indexing in AgentDB.

bestPractices: Use appropriate memory types: episodic for events, semantic for facts, procedural for skills
Implement TTL-based expiration to manage memory size and relevance
Test memory retrieval performance with realistic data volumes before deployment
antiPatterns: Storing all data as single memory type reduces retrieval accuracy and relevance
Skipping memory consolidation leads to unbounded storage growth over time
Hardcoding memory limits prevents scalability and dynamic resource allocation

faq.question: Is this compatible with all AgentDB versions?
faq.answer: Works with AgentDB 2.0 and later versions with memory extension enabled

faq.question: What is the maximum memory size?
faq.answer: Limited by your AgentDB configuration, typically supports millions of vectors

faq.question: Can I integrate with existing databases?
faq.answer: Yes, AgentDB supports hybrid storage combining vector and traditional databases

faq.question: Is user data secure in memory?
faq.answer: AgentDB provides encryption at rest and in transit for sensitive data

faq.question: What if memory retrieval is slow?
faq.answer: Use HNSW indexing and caching layers to achieve sub-50ms retrieval times

faq.question: How does this compare to Redis or MongoDB?
faq.answer: AgentDB combines vector similarity search with traditional storage optimized for AI memory patterns
